answer: The capital city of Brazil is Brasília. It became the capital in 1960, replacing Rio de Janeiro. Brasília is known for its modernist architecture.

results.title: Capitals of Brazil - Wikipedia
results.snippet: The current capital of Brazil, since its construction in 1960, is Brasília. Rio de Janeiro was the country's capital between 1763 and 1960.

results.title: Four Capitals of Brazil | Albuquerque International Association and Center for International Studies
results.snippet: Rio de Janeiro became the capital of colonial Brazil during its most dynamic development in the 19th century, and it remained capital throughout its serving as seat of the Portuguese empire, as capital of the Brazilian empire, and as capital of the Republic of Brazil, until the mid-twentieth century. Alongside these three official capitals, from early in the 20th century, São Paulo emerges as the major industrial center of Latin America and as the financial capital of Brazil and of Latin America as a whole. Today São Paulo, while Rio remains the historical capital of Brazil and the capital of the tourist imaginary of Brazil, serves as the cultural capital of the country. Foster conducted a seminar in 2010 on *Brazilian Urban Fiction* as part of the National Endowment for the Humanities Summer Seminars for College and University Teachers, and in 2007 he conducted a seminar, in Argentina, on Jewish Buenos Aires.
